fact: March 24 is exactly 83 days into the year - that's almost a quarter of the whole year gone already!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees typically start getting their sap tapped to make syrup - talk about tree vampires!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets almost exactly due west around this time, making it super easy to find directions without a compass.
March 24 falls in the middle of Aries season in the zodiac, when the ram constellation is prominent in astrology.
Frogs in temperate regions are often very active right now, croaking loudly to find mates after their long winter rest.
Day and night are still almost perfectly equal everywhere on Earth, since we're just a few days past the spring equinox.
In many northern places, this is often a good time for seeing the Northern Lights because of increased solar activity during equinox season.
Cherry blossom trees in Japan typically bloom around this time, creating spectacular pink flower displays.
March 24 is when many hibernating animals like bears are fully awake and very hungry after months of sleeping.
In the Southern Hemisphere, leaves are usually turning brilliant reds and oranges right now as autumn kicks into high gear.
